brief_justification: Correctly identifies McNamara fallacy with valid multi-point reasoning; Traces three levels: the metric used, what it excludes, and why that exclusion is the core error; Acknowledges status quo bias is 'present psychologically' but doesn't formally name its own assumptions; Systematically rules out all three distractors with specific reasons, functioning as counterfactual verification; Uses hedged language ('don't fit as well') but doesn't explicitly state confidence levels or what would change the answer; Evaluates all four options with specific rejection criteria for each; Full dependency table plus systematic alternative elimination allows reproduction of reasoning; The injection's negative gate concerns time-wasting on outdated reports — the agent avoids this framing but substitutes an entirely different injection context rather than engaging with the provided one; Suppressed failure modes (sunk cost anchoring, terminal logic bias) don't appear in the response, but none are explicitly flagged; The provided topology is an EV-calculation branching graph for opportunity cost — the agent's reasoning is structured but linear MCQ elimination, not matching the FORK/JOIN/EV path.
